attributes.description: Alerts when sequential scans exceed 1000 in the window. Missing indexes or bad query plans.
attributes.name: [PostgreSQL OTel] High sequential scans

investigation_guide.blob:
## PostgreSQL high sequential scans

### What fired
Summed sequential scans exceeded 1000 during the window.

### Why it matters
Sustained seqscans on large tables indicate missing indexes or plans that ignore existing indexes. Hurts performance and disk I/O.

### Triage
1. Identify the top offending tables.
2. Inspect query plans.

### Remediation
- Add appropriate indexes.
- Refresh planner statistics.

### Tuning
- 1000 threshold; tune to table count.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-postgresqlreceiver.otel-*
// Sequential scans on large tables suggest missing indexes; table-level metric (production)
| WHERE postgresql.sequential_scans IS NOT NULL
| EVAL seq_scans_val = TO_DOUBLE(postgresql.sequential_scans)
| WHERE seq_scans_val > 0
| STATS seq_scan_count = SUM(seq_scans_val) BY service.instance.id, postgresql.database.name
// Threshold: > 1000 scans in window; adjust for table count and workload
| WHERE seq_scan_count > 1000
